flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.
